pubmed_links: [Grove et al. (2019) Identification of common genetic risk variants for autism spectrum disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30804558/)
summary: SDK1 encodes a large immunoglobulin superfamily cell adhesion molecule involved in neural circuit formation; genome-wide association studies have implicated SDK1 polymorphisms in autism spectrum disorder and ADHD, and the gene is a transcriptionally active common fragile site in neural stem cells. (emerging evidence)
